description_long: This is a vintage-style portable record player with a circular wooden case and a hinged lid that opens to reveal the turntable mechanism inside. The exterior is finished in a rich, medium-brown wood grain, with metallic hinges and latches securing the lid. The front features a textured handle for carrying, as well as ventilation slats and a gold-colored knob, likely for volume or power control. Inside, a black turntable platter holds a vinyl record, and a silver and black tonearm with a visible stylus rests on its support. The overall design is compact and retro, evoking classic portable record players from the mid-20th century. The object combines both functional and decorative elements, making it suitable for display or use in urban environments such as cafes, parks, or community spaces.
description: A vintage-style portable record player with a round wooden case, hinged lid, and visible turntable mechanism. Features include a carrying handle, control knob, and a classic retro design.
description_view_0: Angled side view showing the open wooden lid, turntable platter, tonearm, and control knob on the left side.
description_view_1: Top-down view of the closed wooden lid, highlighting the circular shape and metal hinges at the back.
description_view_2: Angled side view from the opposite side, showing the open lid, turntable platter with a vinyl record, and ventilation slats on the front.
description_view_3: Frontal view with the lid open, displaying the turntable platter, tonearm, control knob, ventilation slats, and carrying handle.
attribute_turntable_platter: The turntable platter is present and prominently visible inside the wooden case, supporting a vinyl record. It is circular, black in color, and occupies a significant portion of the interior space. The surface appears smooth with a slight sheen, and the record on top has a central label with text. No additional grooves or decorative patterns are visible on the platter itself.
attribute_tonearm: The tonearm is present, extending from the right side of the interior towards the center of the platter. It is straight, medium-length, and features a two-tone color scheme of silver and black. The tonearm is resting on its support, with visible joints and a pivot mechanism at the base.
attribute_stylus: The stylus is visible at the end of the tonearm, appearing as a small, pointed component. It is attached to the tonearm and seems to be a separate, distinct part, likely made of metal or plastic.
attribute_control_knobs: One prominent control knob is visible on the front left side of the case. It is round, gold-colored, and appears to be made of metal or plastic. No visible labels or markings are present, and the knob is likely used for power or volume control.
attribute_dust_cover: The dust cover is present as a solid wooden lid, matching the rest of the case. It is opaque, medium-brown in color, and attached to the base with two metal hinges at the back. The lid is shown in an open position and features two silver latches for securing it when closed.
